---
title: "ggnomics: Significance brackets (geom_signif)"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")

np.random.seed(42)

# Three-group dataset: C is clearly different from A
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "group": ["A"] * 60 + ["B"] * 60 + ["C"] * 60,
    "value": np.concatenate([
        np.random.normal(0, 1, 60),
        np.random.normal(1, 1, 60),
        np.random.normal(3, 1, 60),
    ]),
})
```

## 1. Automatic mode — star annotations on violin

```{python}
from ggnomics import plot_violin_stats

p = plot_violin_stats(
    df, "value", group_by="group",
    comparisons=[("A", "B"), ("A", "C"), ("B", "C")],
    annotation="stars",
    sig_only=False,
)
p
```

## 2. Automatic mode — raw p-value labels

```{python}
p = plot_violin_stats(
    df, "value", group_by="group",
    comparisons=[("A", "C")],
    annotation="pvalue",
    sig_only=False,
)
p
```

## 3. Automatic mode — adjusted p-value labels

```{python}
p = plot_violin_stats(
    df, "value", group_by="group",
    comparisons=[("A", "B"), ("A", "C"), ("B", "C")],
    annotation="padj",
    sig_only=False,
    p_adjust="bonferroni",
)
p
```

## 4. sig_only=True — hide non-significant pairs

```{python}
p = plot_violin_stats(
    df, "value", group_by="group",
    comparisons=[("A", "B"), ("A", "C")],
    annotation="stars",
    sig_only=True,
)
p
```

## 5. Automatic mode on boxplot

```{python}
from ggnomics import plot_box_stats

p = plot_box_stats(
    df, "value", group_by="group",
    annotation="stars",
    sig_only=False,
)
p
```

## 6. Custom annotation function

```{python}
p = plot_violin_stats(
    df, "value", group_by="group",
    comparisons=[("A", "C")],
    annotation=lambda p: f"p={p:.2e}",
    sig_only=False,
)
p
```

## 7. Manual mode — user-built ggplot with pre-computed labels

```{python}
from plotnine import ggplot, aes, geom_boxplot, theme_classic, labs
from ggnomics import geom_signif

p = (
    ggplot(df, aes("group", "value", fill="group"))
    + geom_boxplot()
    + theme_classic()
    + labs(x="Group", y="Value")
)

layers = geom_signif(
    annotations=["***", "ns"],
    y_position=[6.0, 7.5],
    xmin=[1, 1],
    xmax=[3, 2],
)
for layer in layers:
    p = p + layer

p
```

## 8. Asymmetric tip lengths

```{python}
from ggnomics.signif._geom import _DeferredSignif

deferred = geom_signif(
    comparisons=[("A", "C")],
    test="mannwhitney",
    tip_length=[0.08, 0.02],   # left tip longer than right
    map_signif_level=True,
)
layers = deferred.resolve(df, x_col="group", y_col="value")

p = (
    ggplot(df, aes("group", "value", fill="group"))
    + geom_boxplot()
    + theme_classic()
)
for layer in layers:
    p = p + layer
p
```

## 9. Many comparisons — five groups, all pairwise, step_increase visible

```{python}
np.random.seed(7)
df5 = pd.DataFrame({
    "group": [g for g in "ABCDE" for _ in range(40)],
    "value": np.concatenate([
        np.random.normal(i * 0.8, 1, 40) for i in range(5)
    ]),
})

deferred = geom_signif(
    comparisons=[("A", "B"), ("A", "C"), ("A", "D"), ("A", "E")],
    test="mannwhitney",
    step_increase=0.15,
    margin_top=0.05,
    map_signif_level=True,
    sig_only=False,
)
layers = deferred.resolve(df5, x_col="group", y_col="value")

from plotnine import scale_y_continuous

y_max = max(b.y_bracket for b in deferred._last_brackets) if deferred._last_brackets else df5["value"].max()

p = (
    ggplot(df5, aes("group", "value", fill="group"))
    + geom_boxplot()
    + theme_classic()
    + scale_y_continuous(limits=(df5["value"].min() - 0.5, y_max + 0.5))
)
for layer in layers:
    p = p + layer
p
```

## 10. Custom map_signif_level with dict threshold mapping

```{python}
deferred = geom_signif(
    comparisons=[("A", "B"), ("A", "C")],
    test="mannwhitney",
    map_signif_level={0.001: "***", 0.01: "**", 0.05: "*"},
    sig_only=False,
)
layers = deferred.resolve(df, x_col="group", y_col="value")

p = (
    ggplot(df, aes("group", "value", fill="group"))
    + geom_boxplot()
    + theme_classic()
)
for layer in layers:
    p = p + layer
p
```
